    <item>
      <title>What's next for Nikola Jokic, Nuggets after stunning collapse vs. Timberwolves?</title>
      <description>Nikola Jokic and the Denver Nuggets suffered an embarrassing playoff series loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves in six games. Minnesota was without star guard Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo for multiple games due to injuries, yet the Nuggets failed to capitalize on the Timberwolves' setbacks.</description>
      <pubDate>01 May 2026 13:42:19 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/whats_next_for_nikola_jokic_nuggets_after_stunning_collapse_vs_timberwolves/s1_13132_43794912</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/whats_next_for_nikola_jokic_nuggets_after_stunning_collapse_vs_timberwolves/s1_13132_43794912</guid>
      <author>King Hunter, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/2/9/29f166c2ea0b4209d9015446f0c2ee7667f3b09e/thumb_16x9/whats-next-nikola-jokic-nuggets-stunning-collapse.jpg">
        <media:credit> Jesse Johnson-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Denver Nuggets center Nikola Jokic (15) looks on against the Minnesota Timberwolves in the second half during Game 6 of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Target Center. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Denver Nuggets</category>
      <category>Nikola Jokic</category>
    </item>

      <title>No Anthony Edwards, no problem: Jaden McDaniels is the Timberwolves' man</title>

      <title>Raptors stay alive on RJ Barrett's wild game-winning three</title>
      <description>Seven years ago, Kawhi Leonard sent the Toronto Raptors to the Eastern Conference Finals with a game-winning shot that took a fortunate bounce through the hoop.</description>

    <item>
      <title>What Magic can learn from collapse in Game 6 vs. Pistons</title>
      <description>One minute into the second half of Game 6, the Orlando Magic had a 24-point lead and were poised to eliminate the top-seeded Detroit Pistons. They proceeded to score the fewest points ever in an NBA playoff half in a 93-79 loss.</description>
      <pubDate>01 May 2026 21:39:34 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/what_magic_can_learn_from_collapse_in_game_6_vs_pistons/s1_13132_43796385</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/what_magic_can_learn_from_collapse_in_game_6_vs_pistons/s1_13132_43796385</guid>
      <author>Sean Keane, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/d/a/daac474db556785bcfbccc21c4ee5839e884d974/thumb_16x9/magic-learn-collapse-game-6-vs-pistons.jpg">
        <media:credit> Jeremy Reper-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Orlando Magic guard Desmond Bane (3) celebrates with Orlando Magic forward Tristan da Silva (23) in the second quarter against the Detroit Pistons during game six of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Kia Center. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Orlando Magic</category>
      <category>Detroit Pistons</category>
      <category>Paolo Banchero</category>
      <category>Cade Cunningham</category>
      <category>Ausar Thompson</category>
      <category>Paul Reed</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Nikola Jokic knows who to blame for Nuggets' epic playoff collapse</title>
      <description>The Denver Nuggets have some tough decisions to make in the offseason. Failing to take down the No. 6 seed Minnesota Timberwolves without Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo and Ayo Dosunmu might be the biggest blemish in Nikola Jokic's postseason career so far. </description>
      <pubDate>01 May 2026 17:59:44 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/nikola_jokic_knows_who_to_blame_for_nuggets_epic_playoff_collapse/s1_13132_43794101</link>

      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/0/5/05054f29489e2cb2175186582a6f5ef2c01698e3/thumb_16x9/nikola-jokic-knows-blame-nuggets-epic-playoff.jpg">

    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Jaden McDaniels backs up his trash talk with magnificent Game 6 performance</title>
      <description>McDaniels talked trash about the Denver Nuggets all series long. Then in Game 6, with three of his team's best players out, he dominated on both ends of the court and made good on all his boasting.</description>
      <pubDate>01 May 2026 09:13:59 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/jaden_mcdaniels_backs_up_his_trash_talk_with_magnificent_game_6_performance/s1_13132_43793010</link>

    <item>
      <title>Knicks clinch series with historically dominant win over Hawks</title>
      <description>The New York Knicks did far more than just win a game or even clinch a playoff series on Thursday night. The New York Knicks put together a masterpiece that will go down in NBA history.  </description>
      <pubDate>01 May 2026 02:08:19 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/knicks_clinch_series_with_historically_dominant_win_over_hawks/s1_13132_43792349</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/knicks_clinch_series_with_historically_dominant_win_over_hawks/s1_13132_43792349</guid>
      <author>Mike J. Asti, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/4/a/4a0279046608a1e03e4fbf77ec5f0911e7b9ada4/thumb_16x9/knicks-clinch-series-historically-dominant-win.jpg">
        <media:credit> Brett Davis-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Knicks forward OG Anunoby (8) drives on Atlanta Hawks forward Mouhamed Gueye (18) en route to his 29-point performance. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>New York Knicks</category>
      <category>Atlanta Hawks</category>
